records.ety: [L. dissonans, -antis, p. pr. of dissonare to disagree in sound, be discordant; dis- + sonare to sound: cf. F. dissonant. See Sonant.]

quotes: With clamor of voices dissonant and loud.

defs: Disagreeing; incongruous; discrepant, -- with from or to.
quotes: What can be dissonant from reason and nature than that a man, naturally inclined to clemency, should show himself unkind and inhuman?
